variables.USER_FOCUS: High-intensity adversarial review for a Rust Ethereum execution client with Mantle and Optimism-specific crates, consensus, storage, networking, RPC, and node-operator safety concerns.
REPOSITORY_PROFILE: Rust Cargo workspace for Reth, a modular Ethereum execution-layer full node implementation.
Primary language: Rust; workspace edition 2024; MSRV is tracked in Cargo.toml and README.
Workspace includes the reth binary, Ethereum crates, Optimism crates, Mantle hardfork crates, consensus, EVM, payload, RPC, P2P networking, storage, pruning, metrics, node, and testing utilities.
Build and checks commonly involve cargo, cargo nextest, clippy/lints, benchmarks, and Ethereum Foundation tests where relevant.
Public contracts: reth CLI behavior, node configuration, Engine API behavior, JSON-RPC behavior, P2P protocol compatibility, database layout/compatibility, crate APIs, metrics, and package features.

Consensus-critical risk areas: block validation, fork activation, hardfork schedule changes, payload building and validation, Engine API edge cases, state root correctness, receipt/log correctness, transaction pool behavior, and EVM execution semantics.
Storage risk areas: MDBX/database compatibility, pruning, staged sync, snapshots/history, static files, migrations, corruption recovery, and backward compatibility for node operators.
Network/RPC risk areas: peer handling, malformed network messages, DoS surfaces, RPC method compatibility, trace/debug APIs, timeouts, rate limits, and error visibility.
Optimism/Mantle risk areas: L2 payload attributes, system transactions, chain spec changes, fork-specific rules, fee accounting, and deviations from upstream Reth/Optimism behavior.
Performance and reliability matter: avoid regressions that increase sync time, memory, database growth, RPC latency, or node restart/recovery risk.
Review should treat consensus, storage, and networking changes as high blast-radius even when tests pass.
